nodes.cmd: uip or jobs list --folder-key {{shared_folder_key}} --state Faulted --output json

nodes._doc: UNWRAP. The aggregate is a wrapper — never the cause. Extract InnerExceptions[0] (the first FQN after the ' ---> ' marker in the stack) and the inner message. If that inner is itself a wrapper (RemoteException / nested AggregateException), recurse here before classifying.

Dispatch on the unwrapped inner exception FQN. FQN match is exact and deterministic — route to the inner exception's owning playbook subtree, carrying the bound inner_fqn/inner_dap_code/inner_message so the target re-classifies from there.

nodes.cause: AggregateException wrapper on {{activity_kind}}; unwrapped inner is an IS GeneralException (code {{inner_dap_code}}): "{{inner_message}}". The aggregate carries no cause — root is the inner GeneralException. Re-classify in the connector-general-exception subtree (DAP-GE table).

AggregateException wrapper on {{activity_kind}}; unwrapped inner is an IS RuntimeException (code {{inner_dap_code}}): "{{inner_message}}". Re-classify in the connector-runtime-exception subtree (DAP-RT table).

AggregateException wrapper; unwrapped inner is an Ipc/CoreIpc RemoteException (token/transport/HTTP boundary): "{{inner_message}}". This inner is itself a wrapper — re-classify in the connector-remote-exception subtree and recurse to its inner.
AggregateException wrapper; unwrapped inner is a NullReferenceException: "{{inner_message}}". Re-classify in the connector-null-reference subtree.

nodes.judgment_q: InnerExceptions[0] ({{inner_fqn}}: "{{inner_message}}") maps to no IS sub-playbook. Read the verbatim innermost concrete exception from the Info stack; if it is a downstream service or non-IS .NET error, name the concrete fault and hand to the matching domain tree or the free-form hypothesis loop. Report each inner exception if several faulted in parallel.
nodes.reason: Unwrapped inner exception FQN is not in the aggregate dispatch table; needs judgment to reach the innermost concrete cause.
Faulted job exception is not a System.AggregateException wrapper. Route to a different IS playbook (e.g. the raw inner exception's playbook) or hand back to triage.
